lorehq
─[2]-Primitives──
Rules Skills Agents Fieldnotes Runbooks
─[3]-Guides──
CommandsCustomizationGuidesPlatform SupportWorking Across Repos
─[4]-Workflow──
Active Work Architecture
─[ Orders + Inventory Field Test ]─ ┐

Orders + Inventory Field Test

Reproducible protocol for validating Lore-vs-raw behavior using the same mock services and exact prompts.

Goal

Produce auditable evidence for:

  • Raw baseline (no Lore framework)
  • Lore baseline and after-capture reuse
  • Cross-tool reuse (Claude <-> OpenCode)

Repos and Roles

  • lore-gotcha-demo/ - public mock APIs used by all runs
  • agent-demo-no-lore/ - control workspace (no Lore hooks/docs)
  • agent-demo-with-lore/ - Lore-enabled test workspace
  • lorehq/ - evidence documentation and run tracking

Start the Mock APIs

From lore-gotcha-demo/:

node orders-service.js
node inventory-service.js

Service base URLs:

  • Orders: http://localhost:8787
  • Inventory: http://localhost:8791

Prompts (Use Exactly)

Primary prompt:

Let's call the orders service and check for orders from last week.

Complex prompt:

See if we have enough inventory to fill all orders from last week.

Vague prompt:

how many orders did we get last week?

Canonical benchmark prompt (use for cost-stable runs):

Let's call the orders service and check for orders from last week.

Output contract:
- Return exactly 3 lines:
  1) order_count=<n>
  2) total_usd=<amount>
  3) endpoint_used=<method and path>
- No table.
- No extra explanation.

If the agent asks for service location, reply exactly:

The service is at http://localhost:8787

If the agent asks for the inventory service, reply exactly:

Also check inventory at http://localhost:8791

If asked about auth, reply exactly:

dude idk

Which Prompt When

  • Use Primary prompt for single-service baseline discovery and normal reuse checks.
  • Use Complex prompt for cross-service reasoning checks (orders + inventory).
  • Use Vague prompt only for lookup-behavior checks (does it search knowledge/work/context before asking).
  • Use Canonical benchmark prompt when comparing costs/tokens across runs (minimizes response-length variance).

Run Order

  1. Start mock APIs in lore-gotcha-demo:
node orders-service.js
node inventory-service.js
  1. agent-demo-no-lore + Claude (Run 0, control baseline) -> Primary prompt
  2. agent-demo-no-lore + Claude new session (Run 0b, control repeat) -> Primary prompt
  3. agent-demo-with-lore + Claude (Run 1, Lore baseline) -> Primary prompt
  4. agent-demo-with-lore + Claude new session (Run 2, Lore after capture) -> Primary prompt
  5. agent-demo-with-lore + OpenCode (Run 3, cross-tool reuse) -> Primary prompt
  6. Reset test knowledge in agent-demo-with-lore
  7. agent-demo-with-lore + OpenCode (Run 4, fresh OpenCode baseline) -> Primary prompt
  8. agent-demo-with-lore + Claude (Run 5, reverse cross-tool reuse) -> Primary prompt
  9. agent-demo-with-lore + OpenCode (Run 6, OpenCode after capture) -> Primary prompt
  10. Optional lookup test (Run 2b style) in Lore repo -> Vague prompt
  11. Complex scenario pass (same run order as above, fresh sessions) -> Complex prompt

After Each Run

  • Capture the session ID.
  • Send me the session ID; I will calculate tokens/context and append the run log in lorehq.

Local Sanity Check (Before Runs)

From lore-gotcha-demo/ with isolated ports:

PORT=9787 node orders-service.js
INVENTORY_PORT=9791 node inventory-service.js

Then in another terminal:

ORDERS_BASE_URL=http://localhost:9787 INVENTORY_BASE_URL=http://localhost:9791 bash verify-results.sh
ORDERS_BASE_URL=http://localhost:9787 INVENTORY_BASE_URL=http://localhost:9791 bash verify-fulfillment.sh

Metrics Per Run

  • Session ID
  • Time to first successful orders response
  • Probe attempts before success
  • Capture outcome: (A) skill/fieldnote, (B) environment, (C) no capture
  • Hook/context bytes from .git/lore-hook-events.jsonl (Lore runs)
  • Token totals when available

Control-Arm Check

Compare Run 0 vs Run 0b to verify native behavior alone does not produce Lore-style persistence speedups.

Session ID Commands

Claude latest session in agent-demo-with-lore:

basename "$(ls -t ~/.claude/projects/-home-andrew-Github-agent-demo-with-lore/*.jsonl | head -n 1)" .jsonl

OpenCode session IDs: read from ~/.local/share/opencode/log/*.log lines with session id=.

Expected Ground Truth (for demo data)

  • Previous calendar week (2026-02-09 to 2026-02-15): 2 orders
  • Last 7 days from 2026-02-20 (2026-02-13 to 2026-02-20): 3 orders
  • Fulfillment for previous calendar week: cannot fully fulfill
    • Required: SKU_A=5, SKU_B=1, SKU_C=4
    • Available: SKU_A=10, SKU_B=1, SKU_C=2
    • Shortage: SKU_C missing 2

Publish Guidance

Publish lore-gotcha-demo/ as the public verification app. Include:

  • orders-service.js
  • inventory-service.js
  • README.md with expected responses and run commands
  • this protocol path from Lore docs for full methodology
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
